 {"id":"bd-1sviv","title":"Unit Tests: All ntm assign strategies (balanced, speed, quality, dependency)","description":"## Test Coverage
Test all ntm assign strategy algorithms besides round-robin.

## Test Cases

### Balanced Strategy
- Distribute evenly when all agents have 0 assignments
- Prefer agents with fewer current assignments
- Handle tie-breaking (use pane order)
- Update scores after each assignment

### Speed Strategy
- Prefer agents with lower context usage
- Score = 1.0 - (context_pct / 100)
- Handle agents at 100% context (score = 0)
- Prefer fresh agents over loaded ones

### Quality Strategy
- Match bead type to agent capabilities
- Claude for refactoring/architecture
- Codex for feature/bug_fix
- Gemini for documentation/review
- Handle unknown bead types (default score)

### Dependency Strategy
- Prioritize high PageRank beads
- Prioritize high unblocks_count beads
- Score = pagerank * 0.5 + unblocks * 0.5
- Sort beads by score before assignment

### Strategy Selection
- Default to balanced
- Parse --strategy flag correctly
- Error on unknown strategy

## Detailed Logging Requirements (Go test)

### Test Structure
```go
func TestBalancedStrategy(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("=== TestBalancedStrategy ===")
    
    // Setup
    t.Log("[SETUP] Creating test agents and beads")
    agents := []Agent{...}
    beads := []Bead{...}
    t.Logf("[SETUP] Agents: %d, Beads: %d", len(agents), len(beads))
    
    // Execute
    t.Log("[EXEC] Running balanced strategy")
    result := BalancedStrategy(beads, agents)
    t.Logf("[EXEC] Result: %d assignments", len(result))
    
    // Verify
    t.Log("[VERIFY] Checking distribution")
    for i, a := range result {
        t.Logf("[VERIFY] Assignment %d: bead=%s -> pane=%d, score=%.2f",
            i, a.BeadID, a.Pane, a.Score)
    }
    
    // Assert
    if len(result) != len(beads) {
        t.Errorf("[FAIL] Expected %d assignments, got %d", len(beads), len(result))
    } else {
        t.Log("[PASS] Assignment count correct")
    }
}
```

### Table-Driven Tests with Logging
```go
func TestStrategyScoring(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name     string
        strategy string
        agents   []Agent
        beads    []Bead
        want     []Assignment
    }{
        {"balanced_even", "balanced", ...},
        {"balanced_uneven", "balanced", ...},
        {"speed_fresh", "speed", ...},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("[TEST] %s", tt.name)
            t.Logf("[INPUT] Strategy=%s, Agents=%d, Beads=%d",
                tt.strategy, len(tt.agents), len(tt.beads))
            
            got := RunStrategy(tt.strategy, tt.beads, tt.agents)
            
            t.Logf("[OUTPUT] Assignments: %d", len(got))
            for i, a := range got {
                t.Logf("[OUTPUT] [%d] bead=%s pane=%d score=%.2f",
                    i, a.BeadID, a.Pane, a.Score)
            }
            
            if !reflect.DeepEqual(got, tt.want) {
                t.Errorf("[FAIL] Mismatch\nwant: %+v\ngot:  %+v", tt.want, got)
            } else {
                t.Log("[PASS] Output matches expected")
            }
        })
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-1t881","title":"Test: Unit tests for LimitDetector pattern matching","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for LimitDetector pattern matching against actual agent output, covering all rate limit variations.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/limit_detector_test.go
package swarm

import (
    "testing"
    "time"
)

func TestLimitDetector_PatternMatching(t *testing.T) {
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    
    // Test all known rate limit patterns
    patterns := []struct {
        name       string
        agentType  string
        output     string
        shouldMatch bool
        matchType   string
    }{
        // Claude Code patterns
        {
            name:       "claude_rate_limit_standard",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "Error: rate limit exceeded. Please wait before trying again.",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_429_error",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "API Error 429: Too Many Requests",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "429",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_token_limit",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "You have exceeded your token limit for this billing period",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "token_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_normal_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "I'll help you implement that function. Let me start by...",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
        
        // Codex patterns
        {
            name:       "codex_rate_limit",
            agentType:  "cod",
            output:     "openai.RateLimitError: Rate limit reached for default-gpt-4",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "codex_quota_exceeded",
            agentType:  "cod",
            output:     "You exceeded your current quota, please check your plan",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "quota",
        },
        
        // Gemini patterns  
        {
            name:       "gemini_resource_exhausted",
            agentType:  "gmi",
            output:     "google.api_core.exceptions.ResourceExhausted: 429 Resource exhausted",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "resource_exhausted",
        },
        {
            name:       "gemini_quota_limit",
            agentType:  "gmi",
            output:     "Quota exceeded for GenerateContent",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "quota",
        },
        
        // Edge cases
        {
            name:       "partial_match",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "The word rate appears but limit does not follow immediately",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
        {
            name:       "case_insensitive",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "RATE LIMIT EXCEEDED",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "multiline_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "Processing...\nWorking...\nError: rate limit exceeded\nRetrying...",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "empty_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range patterns {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Agent: %s", tt.agentType)
            t.Logf("Output: %q", truncateForLog(tt.output, 100))
            t.Logf("Expected match: %v", tt.shouldMatch)
            
            match, pattern := detector.CheckOutput(tt.agentType, tt.output)
            
            t.Logf("Result: match=%v, pattern=%q", match, pattern)
            
            if tt.shouldMatch {
                assert.True(t, match, "Should detect rate limit")
                assert.NotEmpty(t, pattern, "Should identify pattern type")
                if tt.matchType != "" {
                    assert.Contains(t, pattern, tt.matchType, "Pattern type mismatch")
                }
            } else {
                assert.False(t, match, "Should NOT detect rate limit")
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestLimitDetector_CapturePane(t *testing.T) {
    // Create mock tmux client
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{
        captureResponses: map[string]string{
            "cc_agents_1:1.0": "Normal agent output here",
            "cc_agents_1:1.1": "Error: rate limit exceeded",
            "cc_agents_1:1.2": "", // Empty pane
        },
    }
    
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    detector.tmux = mockTmux
    
    tests := []struct {
        paneID      string
        expectLimit bool
    }{
        {"cc_agents_1:1.0", false},
        {"cc_agents_1:1.1", true},
        {"cc_agents_1:1.2", false},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.paneID, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Capturing pane: %s", tt.paneID)
            
            output, err := detector.CaptureAndCheck(tt.paneID, "cc")
            require.NoError(t, err)
            
            t.Logf("Captured output length: %d", len(output))
            t.Logf("Limit detected: %v", tt.expectLimit)
            
            match, _ := detector.CheckOutput("cc", output)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expectLimit, match)
        })
    }
}

func TestLimitDetector_BackgroundMonitoring(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{}
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    detector.tmux = mockTmux
    detector.pollInterval = 50 * time.Millisecond
    
    t.Log("Starting background monitoring")
    
    ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 200*time.Millisecond)
    defer cancel()
    
    eventCh := detector.Events()
    
    // Start monitoring
    go detector.StartMonitoring(ctx, []PaneSpec{
        {Index: 1, AgentType: "cc"},
    }, "test_session")
    
    // Inject rate limit after short delay
    go func() {
        time.Sleep(75 * time.Millisecond)
        mockTmux.SetCaptureResponse("test_session:1.1", "rate limit exceeded")
    }()
    
    // Wait for event
    select {
    case event := <-eventCh:
        t.Logf("Event received: %+v", event)
        assert.Equal(t, "cc", event.AgentType)
        assert.Contains(t, event.Pattern, "rate")
    case <-ctx.Done():
        t.Log("Context cancelled before event (expected if timing varies)")
    }
}

// Helpers
func truncateForLog(s string, max int) string {
    if len(s) <= max {
        return s
    }
    return s[:max] + "..."
}
```

 {"id":"bd-1uiu4","title":"Task: Unit tests for encryption library","description":"## Scope
Write comprehensive unit tests for the AES-GCM encryption helper.

## Cases
- Round-trip encrypt/decrypt with known key.
- Wrong key returns a typed/recognizable error.
- Corrupted header / corrupted ciphertext returns a typed/recognizable error.

## What It Does
Track agent effectiveness over time and use that data to improve assignments and workflows.

## Architecture (Existing + Planned)
- **Metrics definitions:** `internal/scoring/metrics.go` (core metrics + weights)
- **Score persistence + trends:** `internal/scoring/tracker.go` (JSONL, retention, trend windows)
- **Integration:** `ntm assign` + dashboard panel + robot/analytics export

## Data Sources (Deterministic)
- Task lifecycle signals (beads: assigned → in_progress → closed)
- Retry counts (re-sent prompts / reassignments)
- Time-to-complete (timestamps in task + session logs)
- Token usage (BudgetTracker / TokenEstimator)
- Quality gates (tests/CI pass, regressions)

## Learning / Scoring Rules
- **Cold start:** fallback to neutral baseline until minimum sample threshold met
- **Stability:** use rolling window + decay (recent scores weighted higher)
- **Fairness:** per-task-type normalization to avoid skew from harder tasks
- **No gaming:** cap extreme values, validate sources, log anomalies

1. Per-agent metrics tracked and persisted (JSONL)
2. Task completion rates calculated
3. Effectiveness scores computed deterministically
4. Scores influence recommendations with explainable rationale
5. Dashboard shows agent rankings + trends
6. Robot mode exports scoring data
7. Unit tests for scoring algorithm (>=80% coverage)
8. E2E tests verify scoring accuracy + recommendation influence

- Scores must be reproducible for the same input set","notes":"## Testing Requirements

 {"id":"bd-adgv","title":"Task: Add --robot-mail-check flag","description":"## Overview

Add `--robot-mail-check` flag for AI orchestrators to check agent inboxes via Agent Mail integration.

## IMPORTANT: API Design Consistency

This command follows the **harmonized** robot mode patterns for tool bridges:
- **Naming**: `--robot-mail-check` (no inline value)
- **Project param**: `--project=PROJ` (standard modifier flag)
- **Agent param**: `--agent=ID` (optional filter modifier)
- **Shared modifiers**: `--limit=N`, `--offset=N`, `--since=DATE`, `--until=DATE` (global flags)
- Consistent with `--robot-xf-search --query=Q`, `--robot-dcg-check --command=CMD`

## Command Interface

```bash
# Check all agent inboxes for a project
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject

# Check specific agent inbox
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --agent=cc_1

# Include full message bodies
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --include-bodies

# Limit and paginate results
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --limit=10 --offset=20

# Only urgent messages
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --urgent-only

# Filter by read status
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --status=unread

# Filter by date range
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --since=2024-01-01 --until=2024-06-30

# Check specific thread
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --thread=TKT-api-design

# Verbose output with extra details
ntm --robot-mail-check --project=myproject --verbose
```

## Output Schema

```json

  "timestamp": "2026-01-22T10:30:00Z",
```

## Modifier Flags

| Flag | Description | Default | Scope |
|------|-------------|---------|-------|
| `--project=PROJ` | Project to check (required) | - | **Required** |
| `--agent=ID` | Filter to specific agent inbox | all | Optional |
| `--thread=ID` | Filter to specific thread | all | Optional |
| `--status=S` | Filter by read status: read, unread, all | all | Optional |
| `--include-bodies` | Include full message bodies | false | Optional |
| `--urgent-only` | Only show urgent messages | false | Optional |
| `--verbose` | Include extra details in output | false | Global shared |

| `--offset=N` | Skip first N messages (pagination) | 0 | Global shared |
| `--since=DATE` | Filter to messages after date (YYYY-MM-DD) | none | Global shared |
| `--until=DATE` | Filter to messages before date (YYYY-MM-DD) | none | Global shared |

```bash
# =============================================================================

# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-MAIL]"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0
SKIPPED=0

log_info()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [INFO]  $*"; }
log_pass()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [PASS]  $*"; ((PASSED++)); }
log_fail()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [FAIL]  $*"; ((FAILED++)); }
log_skip()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [SKIP]  $*"; ((SKIPPED++)); }
log_debug() { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [DEBUG] $*"; }

cleanup() {
    log_info "Test summary: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed, $SKIPPED skipped"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log_info "Starting robot mail-check E2E tests"
log_info "Test timestamp: $(date -Iseconds)"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Checking prerequisites..."

# Check if Agent Mail is running (default port 8765)
if ! curl -s http://localhost:8765/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_skip "Agent Mail not running on localhost:8765 - skipping all tests"
    exit 77
fi

log_info "Agent Mail health check passed"

    log_fail "jq is required for tests"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
```

 {"id":"bd-aklq2","title":"Feature: CLI Commands - ntm ensemble command family","description":"# Feature: CLI Commands - Ensemble-first interface

**Users pick goals, not modes.** The primary CLI interface is ensemble selection.

## Primary Commands

### ntm ensemble [ensemble-name] [problem]
Run a named ensemble:
```bash
ntm ensemble project-diagnosis 'What are the main issues with this codebase?'
ntm ensemble idea-forge 'What features should we add next?'
ntm ensemble spec-critique 'Review the API specification'
ntm ensemble safety-risk 'Analyze security concerns'
```

### ntm ensemble list
Show available ensembles with descriptions:
```
Available Ensembles:

project-diagnosis   Systems analysis, red-team, failure modes
idea-forge          Divergent brainstorming, innovation
spec-critique       Requirements review, edge cases
safety-risk         Threat modeling, compliance
architecture-review Multi-perspective arch analysis
tech-debt-triage    Dependency analysis, prioritization
bug-hunt            Multi-angle bug detection
root-cause          Causal + counterfactual analysis
strategic-planning  Long-term planning under uncertainty

Use 'ntm ensemble [name] --help' for details
```

### ntm ensemble [name] --help
Show ensemble details (modes, strategy, typical budget).

### ntm ensemble status [session]
Check running ensemble status (uses existing status patterns).

### ntm ensemble synthesize [session]
Trigger synthesis for completed ensemble.

## Advanced Commands (Power Users)

### Custom modes
```bash
# Explicit mode list by id or code
ntm ensemble --modes=A1,B13,F43 --question 'Analyze the auth system'
```

### ntm ensemble modes

```bash
ntm ensemble modes                    # List core modes (default)
ntm ensemble modes --all              # Include advanced
ntm ensemble modes --category formal  # Filter by category
ntm ensemble modes --verbose          # Full mode details
```

### ntm ensemble presets
Alias for `ntm ensemble list` (shared implementation).

## Flags (CLI)
Common flags across ensemble commands:
```
--agents string      Override agent distribution (cc=3,cod=2,gmi=1)
--assignment string  round-robin|affinity|explicit
--allow-advanced     Explicit opt-in for advanced/experimental modes
--tier string        core|advanced|experimental|all (list/modes only)
--format string      table|json|yaml (list/modes/presets/status/synthesize)
--output string      Write synthesis to file (synthesize only)
--no-questions       Skip targeted questions (use auto-context only)
--no-cache           Bypass context pack cache
--budget-total int   Override total tokens
--budget-per-agent int Override per-agent cap
```

 {"id":"bd-h3ha","title":"Add ntm send --randomize - randomize order of individualized prompts to avoid thundering herd on same files","description":"## Overview
Add --randomize flag to ntm send to randomize the order of individualized prompts, avoiding thundering herd problems when multiple agents start working on overlapping files.

## Problem
When sending individualized prompts (e.g., each agent gets a different bead), agents often start simultaneously on related files. This causes:
- Git conflicts when multiple agents edit the same area
- File reservation conflicts in Agent Mail
- Wasted work from merge conflicts

## Solution
`ntm send myproject --randomize` shuffles the order of prompts before sending.

## Implementation
```go
func randomizePrompts(prompts []Prompt) []Prompt {
    shuffled := make([]Prompt, len(prompts))
    copy(shuffled, prompts)
    rand.Shuffle(len(shuffled), func(i, j int) {
        shuffled[i], shuffled[j] = shuffled[j], shuffled[i]
    })
    return shuffled
}
```

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Randomize prompt order
ntm send myproject --randomize

# Combine with batch
ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt --randomize

# Combine with delay for extra staggering  
ntm send myproject --randomize --delay 2s
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prompts shuffled before sending
- [ ] Deterministic seed option for reproducibility (--seed=N)
- [ ] Works with --batch and --distribute
- [ ] Logging shows shuffle order
- [ ] No effect if only one prompt

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Shuffle produces different order
- Unit test: Seed produces reproducible order
- E2E test: Randomized batch send","notes":"## Testing Requirements

```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# scripts/test_swarm_e2e.sh
# End-to-end test for weighted multi-project agent swarm orchestration
#
# Prerequisites:
# - ntm binary built and in PATH
# - tmux installed
# - br (beads_rust) installed
# - Test project directories set up

set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# Configuration
# ============================================================================
TEST_DIR="${TEST_DIR:-/tmp/ntm_e2e_test_$$}"
LOG_FILE="${TEST_DIR}/e2e_test.log"
VERBOSE="${VERBOSE:-1}"

# Colors for output
RED="\033[0;31m"
GREEN="\033[0;32m"
YELLOW="\033[1;33m"
NC="\033[0m" # No Color

# ============================================================================
# Logging Functions
# ============================================================================
log() {
    local level=$1
    shift
    local msg="$*"
    local timestamp=$(date "+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N")
    echo -e "[$timestamp] [$level] $msg" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_info()  { log "INFO " "$@"; }
log_warn()  { log "WARN " "${YELLOW}$*${NC}"; }
log_error() { log "ERROR" "${RED}$*${NC}"; }
log_pass()  { log "PASS " "${GREEN}$*${NC}"; }
log_fail()  { log "FAIL " "${RED}$*${NC}"; }

log_section() {
    echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    echo "============================================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    echo "  $1" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    echo "============================================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

# ============================================================================
# Setup Functions
# ============================================================================
setup_test_environment() {
    log_section "Setting up test environment"
    
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR"
    log_info "Test directory: $TEST_DIR"
    log_info "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
    
    # Create test projects with different bead counts
    log_info "Creating test projects..."
    
    # Tier 1 project (400+ beads)
    create_test_project "tier1-project" 450
    
    # Tier 2 project (100-399 beads)
    create_test_project "tier2-project" 200
    
    # Tier 3 project (<100 beads)
    create_test_project "tier3-project" 50
    
    # Project without beads (should be skipped)
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/no-beads-project"
    log_info "Created no-beads-project (should be skipped)"
    
    log_pass "Test environment setup complete"
}

create_test_project() {
    local name=$1
    local bead_count=$2
    local project_dir="$TEST_DIR/$name"
    
    mkdir -p "$project_dir/.beads"
    
    # Create mock beads
    for i in $(seq 1 $bead_count); do
        echo "{\"id\":\"bd-$i\",\"status\":\"open\",\"title\":\"Test bead $i\"}" >> "$project_dir/.beads/issues.jsonl"
    done
    
    log_info "Created $name with $bead_count beads"
}

# ============================================================================
# Test Cases
# ============================================================================
test_dry_run() {
    log_section "Test: Dry Run Mode"
    
    log_info "Running: ntm swarm --scan-dir $TEST_DIR --dry-run"
    
    local output
    output=$(ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --dry-run 2>&1) || {
        log_fail "Dry run command failed"
        log_error "Output: $output"
        return 1
    }
    
    log_info "Dry run output:"
    echo "$output" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    
    # Verify expected output
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "tier1-project"; then
        log_pass "Tier 1 project detected"
    else
        log_fail "Tier 1 project NOT detected"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "tier2-project"; then
        log_pass "Tier 2 project detected"
    else
        log_fail "Tier 2 project NOT detected"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "no-beads-project"; then
        log_fail "No-beads project should NOT appear"
        return 1
    else
        log_pass "No-beads project correctly excluded"
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "Dry-run complete"; then
        log_pass "Dry run completed without execution"
    else
        log_fail "Dry run indicator not found"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Dry run test PASSED"
}

test_plan_output() {
    log_section "Test: Plan JSON Output"
    
    local plan_file="$TEST_DIR/swarm_plan.json"
    
    log_info "Running: ntm swarm --scan-dir $TEST_DIR --dry-run --output $plan_file"
    
    ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --dry-run --output "$plan_file" 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    
    if [[ \! -f "$plan_file" ]]; then
        log_fail "Plan file not created"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_info "Plan file created: $plan_file"
    log_info "Plan contents:"
    cat "$plan_file" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    
    # Validate JSON structure
    if \! jq -e . "$plan_file" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_fail "Plan file is not valid JSON"
        return 1
    fi
    log_pass "Plan file is valid JSON"
    
    # Check required fields
    local total_agents
    total_agents=$(jq -r ".total_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Total agents in plan: $total_agents"
    
    if [[ "$total_agents" -gt 0 ]]; then
        log_pass "Plan contains agent allocations"
    else
        log_fail "Plan has no agent allocations"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Plan output test PASSED"
}

test_session_creation() {
    log_section "Test: Session Creation (requires tmux)"
    
    # Skip if tmux not available
    if \! command -v tmux &> /dev/null; then
        log_warn "tmux not available, skipping session creation test"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Clean up any existing test sessions
    tmux kill-session -t "cc_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    log_info "Running swarm with single session for test..."
    log_info "ntm swarm --scan-dir $TEST_DIR --sessions-per-type 1 --dry-run"
    
    # For actual execution (commented out for safety):
    # ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --sessions-per-type 1
    
    # Verify sessions would be created
    local plan_file="$TEST_DIR/session_test_plan.json"
    ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --sessions-per-type 1 --dry-run --output "$plan_file"
    
    local session_count
    session_count=$(jq -r ".sessions | length" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Sessions in plan: $session_count"
    
    if [[ "$session_count" -ge 3 ]]; then
        log_pass "Plan includes expected session count (3 types)"
    else
        log_fail "Unexpected session count: $session_count"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check session naming
    local cc_session
    cc_session=$(jq -r ".sessions[] | select(.agent_type==\"cc\") | .name" "$plan_file" | head -1)
    log_info "CC session name: $cc_session"
    
    if [[ "$cc_session" == "cc_agents_1" ]]; then
        log_pass "Session naming is correct"
    else
        log_fail "Session naming mismatch: expected cc_agents_1, got $cc_session"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Session creation test PASSED"
}

test_allocation_tiers() {
    log_section "Test: Allocation Tier Calculations"
    
    local plan_file="$TEST_DIR/tier_test_plan.json"
    ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --dry-run --output "$plan_file"
    
    # Check tier 1 allocation
    local tier1_cc
    tier1_cc=$(jq -r ".allocations[] | select(.project.name==\"tier1-project\") | .cc_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Tier 1 (450 beads) CC allocation: $tier1_cc"
    
    # Check tier 2 allocation  
    local tier2_cc
    tier2_cc=$(jq -r ".allocations[] | select(.project.name==\"tier2-project\") | .cc_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Tier 2 (200 beads) CC allocation: $tier2_cc"
    
    # Check tier 3 allocation
    local tier3_cc
    tier3_cc=$(jq -r ".allocations[] | select(.project.name==\"tier3-project\") | .cc_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Tier 3 (50 beads) CC allocation: $tier3_cc"
    
    # Verify tier hierarchy
    if [[ "$tier1_cc" -gt "$tier2_cc" ]] && [[ "$tier2_cc" -ge "$tier3_cc" ]]; then
        log_pass "Tier allocation hierarchy is correct"
    else
        log_fail "Tier allocation hierarchy violated: T1=$tier1_cc, T2=$tier2_cc, T3=$tier3_cc"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Allocation tier test PASSED"
}

# ============================================================================
# Cleanup
# ============================================================================
cleanup() {
    log_section "Cleanup"
    
    # Kill any test tmux sessions
    tmux kill-session -t "cc_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    tmux kill-session -t "cod_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    tmux kill-session -t "gmi_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    if [[ "${KEEP_TEST_DIR:-0}" == "0" ]]; then
        log_info "Removing test directory: $TEST_DIR"
        rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
    else
        log_info "Keeping test directory: $TEST_DIR"
    fi
    
    log_info "Cleanup complete"
}

# ============================================================================
# Main
# ============================================================================
main() {
    log_section "NTM Swarm E2E Test Suite"
    log_info "Starting at $(date)"
    log_info "Test directory: $TEST_DIR"
    
    trap cleanup EXIT
    
    local failed=0
    
    setup_test_environment
    
    test_dry_run || ((failed++))
    test_plan_output || ((failed++))
    test_allocation_tiers || ((failed++))
    test_session_creation || ((failed++))
    
    log_section "Test Summary"
    if [[ $failed -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_pass "All tests PASSED"
        exit 0
    else
        log_fail "$failed test(s) FAILED"
        exit 1
    fi
}

main "$@"
```
